drugs.notes: Intravenous infusion in 100ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 6 to 8 hours. Premedicate with chlorphenamine 10mg IV and paracetamol 1000mg oral 30 minutes before. Irradiated blood products required for life.

Intravenous infusion in 100ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 6 to 8 hours. Premedicate with chlorphenamine 10mg IV and paracetamol 1000mg oral 30 minutes before. Irradiated blood products required for life.

12 doses total, given every 6 hours starting at 1000hrs on day -8 and finishing at 0400hrs on day -5. Diluted to 0.5mg/ml in sodium chloride 0.9%. Dose rounded to nearest 3mg. Obese patients: use adjusted ideal body weight. TDM required. Clonazepam 500mcg oral twice daily starting the night before busulfan and continuing until 3 days after last dose.
Intravenous infusion in 50ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes. Dose rounded to nearest 2.5mg.

GvHD prophylaxis. IV bolus over 5 minutes. Administer at 1700hrs. Dose rounded to nearest 2.5mg. Check bilirubin, creatinine and mucositis before each dose. Calcium folinate 30mg IV bolus 6-hourly for 4 doses starting 24 hours after each methotrexate dose (days +4, +7, +12).

GvHD prophylaxis. IV bolus over 5 minutes. Administer at 1700hrs. Dose rounded to nearest 2.5mg. Check bilirubin, creatinine and mucositis before each dose. Calcium folinate 30mg IV bolus 6-hourly for 4 doses starting 24 hours after each methotrexate dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment: serum bilirubin greater than 85 µmol/L

dose_modifications.notes: Caution in severe hepatic impairment; consider dose reduction with raised liver enzymes. Clinical decision.

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment: CrCl less than 30 ml/min
Renal impairment: serum creatinine greater than 180 µmol/L
Mucositis NCI-CTC grade 3 or more on day +11
dose_modifications.notes: Day +11 methotrexate dose may be reduced or omitted. Discuss with transplant clinician.

Paracetamol 1000mg oral 30 minutes prior to alemtuzumab and stem cells
Pethidine 12.5-25mg IV if required for alemtuzumab-induced rigors (under doctor supervision)
Clonazepam 500mcg oral twice daily (start night before busulfan, continue until 3 days after last dose)
Metoclopramide 10mg three times a day oral or IV (antiemetic)
Ondansetron 8mg twice a day oral or IV (antiemetic)
Ciclosporin oral or IV (GvHD prophylaxis - prescribed separately on inpatient system)
Calcium folinate 30mg IV bolus 6-hourly for 4 doses starting 24 hours after each methotrexate dose (days +4, +7, +12)
special_warnings: Irradiated blood products required for life following alemtuzumab
Busulfan: 12 doses given every 6 hours over days -8 to -5; TDM (therapeutic drug monitoring) required
Clonazepam seizure prophylaxis mandatory during busulfan
GFR measurement by Nuclear Medicine required prior to first day of treatment
FBC, LFTs and U&Es required prior to initiating treatment
LFTs and creatinine required prior to each methotrexate dose
Evaluate mucositis prior to each methotrexate administration
Regimen only available at Wessex Blood and Marrow Transplant Unit
Busulfan dose rounded to nearest 3mg; dose banding tables not applicable
Obese patients: busulfan dosing based on adjusted ideal body weight

drugs.notes: Intravenous infusion in 100ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 6 to 8 hours. Premedicate with chlorphenamine 10mg IV and paracetamol 1000mg oral 30 minutes before. Irradiated blood products required for life.

Intravenous infusion in 100ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 6 to 8 hours. Premedicate with chlorphenamine 10mg IV and paracetamol 1000mg oral 30 minutes before. Irradiated blood products required for life.

Intravenous infusion in 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 2 hours. Dose banded per national dose banding table (20mg/ml). Mesna uroprotection 24mg/kg IV required (doses at Day -6: 1000, 1300, 1600, 1900, 2200hrs; Day -5: 0200, 0600, 1000, 1300, 1600, 1900, 2200hrs; Day -4: 0200, 0600hrs). IV hydration required before and after infusion. Haemorrhagic cystitis risk.

165 cGy per fraction, given twice daily on days -3, -2, -1 and 0 (total 8 fractions = 1320 cGy total). Premedicate with dexamethasone 4mg IV and ondansetron 8mg oral/IV prior to each fraction. Lorazepam 1mg oral twice daily starting the night before radiotherapy and continuing until radiotherapy complete.

GvHD prophylaxis. IV bolus over 5 minutes. Administer at 1700hrs. Dose rounded to nearest 2.5mg. Check bilirubin, creatinine and mucositis before each dose. Calcium folinate 30mg IV bolus 6-hourly for 4 doses starting 24 hours after each methotrexate dose (days +4, +7, +12).

GvHD prophylaxis. IV bolus over 5 minutes. Administer at 1700hrs. Dose rounded to nearest 2.5mg. Check bilirubin, creatinine and mucositis before each dose. Calcium folinate 30mg IV bolus 6-hourly for 4 doses starting 24 hours after each methotrexate dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment: serum bilirubin greater than 85 µmol/L

dose_modifications.notes: 25% dose reduction recommended. Severe hepatic impairment may decrease activation of cyclophosphamide.

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment: CrCl less than 30 ml/min
dose_modifications.notes: High dose therapy and stem cell transplantation generally not undertaken

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment: serum creatinine greater than 180 µmol/L

Dexamethasone 4mg IV or equivalent prior to each TBI fraction

Mesna 24mg/kg IV (uroprotection for cyclophosphamide)

IV hydration prescribed on inpatient system before and after cyclophosphamide

Irradiated blood products required for life following alemtuzumab
Cyclophosphamide: haemorrhagic cystitis risk; mesna uroprotection mandatory; extensive IV hydration required days -7 to -4

Intravenous infusion in 50ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes. Dose rounded to nearest 2.5mg.

Intravenous infusion in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes. Short expiry - administer at 1100hrs. Dose rounded to nearest 10mg. Pre-hydration and post-hydration protocol mandatory (see appendix). Urine output must be >500ml before proceeding with infusion. Furosemide 20mg IV bolus given prior to infusion to ensure adequate diuresis.

protocol_name: HSCT Cyclophosphamide Priming (1500mg/m²) - Stem Cell Mobilisation

drugs.notes: Intravenous bolus in 100ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 15 minutes. Start at same time as cyclophosphamide infusion (uroprotection). Dose banded per national dose banding tables.

Intravenous infusion in 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 120 minutes. Dose banded per national dose banding tables (20mg/ml). Pre-hydration: 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% IV over 120 minutes. Patients must drink 2-3 litres fluid on day of chemotherapy and day after. Haemorrhagic cystitis risk. Urine dip for protein every 60 minutes until end of infusion.
Two oral doses: first dose immediately at end of cyclophosphamide infusion, second dose 4 hours after end of infusion. Rounded to nearest 400mg. If vomiting, administer IV instead.

dose_modifications.notes: Only patients with CrCl >40ml/min should be considered for this regimen

pre_medications: Dexamethasone 8mg oral or equivalent IV bolus 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy
Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV bolus 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy
Mesna 300mg/m² IV at same time as cyclophosphamide
Pre-hydration: 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% IV over 120 minutes prior to cyclophosphamide
Take home: Metoclopramide 10mg three times a day when required oral
Take home: Ondansetron 8mg twice a day oral for 3 days
Growth factors (filgrastim or lenograstim) starting day 2 until adequate cell count harvest
Anti-infective prophylaxis for 11 days: aciclovir 400mg twice daily, ciprofloxacin 250mg twice daily, fluconazole 100mg once daily
special_warnings: Haematopoietic stem cell mobilisation (autograft priming) - NOT allograft conditioning
Irradiated blood products required: from 2 weeks before cyclophosphamide until 12 months post-treatment due to TA-GVHD risk
Transfusion departments must be notified and patient issued alert card
Only patients with CrCl >40ml/min should be considered
Urine dip for protein every 60 minutes until end of cyclophosphamide infusion
Haemorrhagic cystitis risk - mesna uroprotection mandatory
Patients must drink 2-3 litres on day of treatment and day after
Contact acute oncology if pain/inability to pass urine, blood in urine, or vomiting within 1 hour of oral mesna

protocol_name: Cyclophosphamide-Total Body Irradiation (TBI)-Methotrexate GvHD (Related Donor Conditioning)

drugs.notes: Intravenous infusion in 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 2 hours. Start at 1015hrs. Dose banded per national dose banding table (20mg/ml). Mesna uroprotection 24mg/kg IV required (Day -6: 1000, 1300, 1600, 1900, 2200hrs; Day -5: 0200, 0600, 1000, 1300, 1600, 1900, 2200hrs; Day -4: 0200, 0600hrs). Extensive IV hydration required days -7 to -4. Haemorrhagic cystitis risk.

165 cGy per fraction, given twice daily on days -3, -2, -1 and 0 (total 8 fractions = 1320 cGy total). Premedicate with dexamethasone 3.3mg IV equivalent twice daily at 0730 and 1600hrs before TBI on days -3, -2, -1. Lorazepam 1mg oral twice daily starting night before radiotherapy and continuing until complete.

GvHD prophylaxis. IV bolus over 5 minutes. Administer at 1700hrs. Dose rounded to nearest 2.5mg. Check bilirubin, creatinine and mucositis before each dose. Calcium folinate 30mg IV bolus 6-hourly for 4 doses starting 24 hours after each methotrexate dose (days +4, +7, +12).

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment: serum bilirubin greater than 85 µmol/L
Renal impairment: CrCl less than 30 ml/min
dose_modifications.notes: High dose therapy and stem cell transplantation generally not undertaken

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment: serum creatinine greater than 180 µmol/L
Mucositis NCI-CTC grade 3 or more on day +11
dose_modifications.notes: Day +11 methotrexate dose may be reduced or omitted. Discuss with transplant clinician.

pre_medications: Metoclopramide 10mg three times a day oral or IV (antiemetic prior to cyclophosphamide)
Ondansetron 8mg twice a day oral or IV (antiemetic prior to cyclophosphamide)
Dexamethasone 4mg IV or equivalent prior to each TBI fraction
Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV prior to each TBI fraction
Dexamethasone 3.3mg IV (or equivalent) twice daily at 0730 and 1600hrs before TBI (days -3, -2, -1)
Lorazepam 1mg oral twice daily starting night before TBI, continuing until TBI complete
Mesna 24mg/kg IV (uroprotection for cyclophosphamide)
Ciclosporin oral or IV (GvHD prophylaxis - prescribed separately on inpatient system)
Calcium folinate 30mg IV bolus 6-hourly for 4 doses starting 24 hours after each methotrexate dose (days +4, +7, +12)
IV hydration prescribed on inpatient system before and after cyclophosphamide (days -7 to -4)
special_warnings: Full intensity conditioning for related donor allograft
Cyclophosphamide: haemorrhagic cystitis risk; mesna uroprotection mandatory; extensive IV hydration required days -7 to -4
TBI: 165 cGy twice daily on days -3, -2, -1 and 0 (total 8 fractions)
Lorazepam premedication mandatory for TBI
GFR measurement by Nuclear Medicine required prior to first day of treatment
FBC, LFTs and U&Es required prior to initiating treatment
LFTs and creatinine required prior to each methotrexate dose
Evaluate mucositis prior to each methotrexate administration
Regimen only available at Wessex Blood and Marrow Transplant Unit
Mesna dose rounded to nearest 100mg

protocol_name: HSCT Melphalan (200mg/m²) - Multiple Myeloma Autograft Conditioning

drugs.notes: Intravenous infusion in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes. Administer at 1100hrs. Short expiry (90 minutes) - must be made locally; strict timing essential. Dose rounded to nearest 10mg. Pre-hydration mandatory (1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% with potassium chloride 27mmol overnight before, completed by 0930 on day of infusion; 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% at 0930hrs over 90 min; furosemide 20mg IV bolus at 1010hrs; urine output >500ml required before proceeding). Extensive post-hydration required.

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment: CrCl less than 30 ml/min
dose_modifications.notes: High dose melphalan not recommended; clinical decision required

pre_medications: Aprepitant 125mg oral once daily on day of melphalan, then 80mg once daily for 2 days afterwards
Dexamethasone 4mg oral each morning on same days as aprepitant
Metoclopramide 10mg three times a day oral or IV until nausea subsides
Ondansetron 8mg twice a day oral or IV for ten days then review
Chlorphenamine 10mg IV (premedication for stem cell transfusion)
Paracetamol 1000mg oral (premedication for stem cell transfusion)
Pre-hydration: 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% with potassium chloride 27mmol overnight (completed by 0930)
Furosemide 20mg IV bolus at 1010hrs to ensure adequate diuresis before melphalan
Growth factors (filgrastim or lenograstim) from day +7 until neutrophils >0.5

Antimicrobials: ciprofloxacin 250mg twice daily from day +5 whilst neutropenic; fluconazole 100mg once daily from admission until neutrophil recovery; aciclovir 400mg two to three times daily from admission
Gastric protection with proton pump inhibitor throughout admission
Thromboprophylaxis as per individual transplant schedule
special_warnings: Autograft conditioning for multiple myeloma
Melphalan 200mg/m²: short expiry (90 minutes) - must be made locally for individual patient; strict timing protocol mandatory
Urine output must exceed 500ml before proceeding with melphalan infusion; if <500ml give second furosemide 20mg IV and recheck
Extensive pre- and post-hydration protocol required
GFR measurement by Nuclear Medicine or creatinine clearance calculation required prior to treatment
FBC, LFTs and U&Es required prior to initiating treatment
No dose modification for hepatic impairment
Dose reduced to 140mg/m² for CrCl 30-50 ml/min
Only available at units which carry out autograft transplantation
